# The amyloid response across tau backgrounds, method by method {#sec-modality}

```{r}
#| label: modality-setup
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
})
options(warn = 2)                                    # promote any chunk warning to a render error (gate-critical)
targets::tar_source()                                # constants, plot theme + modality figure helpers
targets::tar_load(modality_scatter_figures)          # compact logFC pairs + functional group scores
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
theme_set(theme_tau())

msf <- modality_scatter_figures
```

## Non-snRNAseq modality-native figures

```{r}
#| label: fig-geomx-qc-atlas
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 7.6
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx AOI QC atlas. Top: library size, detected genes, nuclei, AOI area, negative background, and Q3 factor by slide, with genotype colour and segment shape; black rings mark the metric-specific QC flags. Bottom: flagged-AOI counts by slide and segment. Flags use empirical tail cutoffs plus the nuclei sentinel and are descriptive only; no AOIs are excluded in this figure."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx quality-control figure showing per-AOI library size, detected genes, nuclei, area, negative background, and Q3 factor by slide, genotype, and segment, plus a flagged-AOI count panel for low-depth, low-detection, nuclei-sentinel, small-area, high-background, and high-Q3 states."
geomx_qc_atlas_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx$qc)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-geomx-normalization-rle
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 7.8
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx normalization and RLE figure. Top left: raw logCPM and TMM-normalized logCPM distributions for the filter-passing GeoMx genes, shown as per-AOI quantile summaries by slide. Top right: TMM relative-log-expression summaries by slide, genotype, and segment. Bottom left: Q3 normalization factor against negative-control background. Bottom right: the voom mean-variance trend from the primary slide-adjusted, bio-unit-blocked GeoMx fit. The DE model remains limma-voom with slide fixed effect and duplicateCorrelation; this figure is diagnostic and excludes no AOIs."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx normalization diagnostic with raw versus TMM logCPM quantiles by slide, TMM RLE by slide and genotype, Q3 normalization factor versus negative-control background, and the voom mean-variance trend from the primary GeoMx model."
geomx_normalization_rle_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx$normalization)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-geomx-ordination
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 9.6
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx ordination figure. Top: PCA and classical MDS of AOIs using TMM-normalized logCPM from the top variable filter-passing genes, with genotype colour and slide facets; point shape encodes segment when multiple segment levels are present. Bottom left: PCA variance explained for the leading components. Bottom right: top signed loading genes for PC1 and PC2. The figure is descriptive, deterministic, and excludes no AOIs."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx ordination diagnostic with slide-faceted PCA and MDS AOI scatter plots coloured by genotype, a PCA scree curve, and signed PC1 and PC2 top loading genes."
geomx_ordination_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx$ordination)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-geomx-gene-detection
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 8.4
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx gene-detection figure. Top left: mean TMM logCPM against the fraction of AOIs with raw count >=5 for every WTA gene, with filterByExpr status and high-detection microglia marker labels. Top right: the low-coverage filter decision across detection-fraction bins. Bottom left: measurable microglia identity, homeostatic, and DAM marker genes. Bottom right: highest-detected filter-passing genes. This diagnostic documents marker measurability and changes no GeoMx exclusions."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx gene-detectability diagnostic showing all-gene mean expression versus detection fraction, the filterByExpr low-coverage decision by detection bins, measurable microglia identity, homeostatic, and DAM markers, and the highest-detected filter-passing genes."
geomx_gene_detection_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx$gene_detection)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-geomx-sample-heatmap
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 10.2
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx sample heatmap. Top: clustered AOI annotation tracks for genotype, slide, segment, bio-unit, and ROI, followed by the signed GeoMx amyloid-response score used in the spatial plate. Bottom: row-z-scored expression for a capped set of top-variable filter-passing GeoMx genes, with AOIs and genes ordered by deterministic average-linkage clustering. This exploratory figure shows sample/gene structure only and changes no GeoMx exclusions."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx exploratory sample heatmap with AOI annotation tracks for genotype, slide, segment, bio-unit, and ROI, a signed amyloid-response score strip, and a clustered z-score heatmap of top-variable filter-passing GeoMx genes."
geomx_sample_heatmap_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx$sample_heatmap)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-geomx-spatial-program-overlays
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 9.8
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx spatial program overlays. AOI coordinate maps show Homeostatic, DAM, IFN, and MHC/APC signature scores plus Apoe and Trem2 single-gene scores, all computed as TMM-logCPM row-z summaries over filter-passing GeoMx genes. The maps are coordinate-only because tissue image files are not in the live report path; rings show genotype, fill shows score, and the bottom panel summarises AOI score medians by genotype. This diagnostic changes no AOI exclusions or DE model."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx coordinate-only spatial overlay figure with slide-faceted AOI maps for homeostatic, DAM, IFN, MHC/APC, Apoe, and Trem2 program scores, plus genotype-level median and interquartile summaries for each program."
geomx_spatial_program_overlay_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx$spatial_programs)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-geomx-landscape
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 7.2
#| fig-cap: "GeoMx spatial AOI plate. Top: AOI coordinates faceted by slide, with signed top-amyloid-response gene score as fill, genotype as point ring, AOI area as point size, and the highest-magnitude ROIs labelled per slide. Bottom left: the same signed score summarised by genotype across AOIs. Bottom right: the top score-gene drivers, showing the GeoMx log2 fold change for the amyloid effect on the tau-KO and mutant-tau backgrounds."
#| fig-alt: "GeoMx figure with a slide-faceted AOI spatial map coloured by signed expression score, a genotype-level score distribution, and a paired top-gene driver plot comparing amyloid effects in MAPTKI and P301S backgrounds."
geomx_spatial_modality_plot(msf$descriptive$GeoMx, title = msf$panels$GeoMx$title)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-proteome-landscape
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 5.4
#| fig-cap: "Bulk proteome descriptive figure. Left: PCA of the 16 median-normalised 24M protein-group intensity profiles, with points labelled by run index. Right: a protein-level volcano plot for the amyloid effect on the mutant-tau background (NLGF_P301S vs P301S), using log2 fold change against -log10 FDR and labelling the top-ranked proteins."
#| fig-alt: "Bulk proteome figure with a genotype-coloured sample PCA and a protein differential-abundance volcano plot for NLGF_P301S versus P301S."
proteome_modality_plot(msf$descriptive$Proteome, title = msf$panels$Proteome$title)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-phospho-landscape
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 5.8
#| fig-cap: "Bulk phosphoproteome descriptive figure. Left: phosphosite-level volcano plot for the amyloid effect on the mutant-tau background (NLGF_P301S vs P301S). Right: a z-scored abundance heatmap for top-ranked phosphosites across the 16 24M runs, grouped by genotype."
#| fig-alt: "Bulk phosphoproteome figure with a phosphosite differential-abundance volcano plot and a genotype-grouped heatmap of top phosphosite abundances across runs."
phospho_modality_plot(msf$descriptive$Phospho, title = "Bulk phosphoproteome (24M)")
```

## Per-method amyloid effect: tau-KO versus mutant-tau background

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-amyloid-effect
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 10.4
#| fig-cap: "Per-method amyloid (NLGF) log2 fold change on the tau-KO background (NLGF_MAPTKI vs MAPTKI, y) against the mutant-tau background (NLGF_P301S vs P301S, x). The dashed line is y=x, the null of a tau-independent amyloid response; the tau-by-amyloid interaction contrast is x minus y, so distance from the diagonal ranks its magnitude. Phosphoproteomics points are parent-protein means of finite phosphosite logFC pairs. Labels mark empirical off-diagonal outliers using a standardized within-method rule: unique display labels whose |x-y| exceeds that method's 99th percentile of |x-y|. The solid line is the OLS trend."
#| fig-alt: "Four scatter plots, one per method, each comparing the amyloid log fold change between the two tau backgrounds against a dashed identity line, with an OLS trend and empirical off-diagonal outliers labelled; phosphoproteomics is collapsed to parent-protein means."
panels <- lapply(msf$order, function(m) {
  modality_interaction_scatter(msf$panels[[m]]$data, title = msf$panels[[m]]$title)
})
wrap_plots(panels, ncol = 2)
```

## Off-diagonal functional categories

```{r}
#| label: fig-modality-functional-scores
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 8.2
#| fig-cap: "Within-method 99th-percentile off-diagonal genes and proteins from the amyloid-effect scatter assigned to one primary functional category per method. Broad role categories are assembled from mouse GO-BP annotations; threshold-passing items without a role-set hit are retained in explicit fallback categories rather than dropped. Phosphoproteomics categories use the same parent-protein mean points displayed in the scatter. Each segment connects the aggregate amyloid log2 fold change in NLGF_MAPTKI to the aggregate amyloid log2 fold change in NLGF_P301S; segment colour is P301S minus MAPTKI, and point size is the number of scored genes or proteins in the category."
#| fig-alt: "Faceted dumbbell plots show modality-specific functional categories for within-method 99th-percentile off-diagonal genes or proteins, with paired points for aggregate NLGF_MAPTKI and NLGF_P301S amyloid responses connected by a segment coloured by the P301S minus MAPTKI score."
functional_group_score_plot(msf$groups$summary, title = "Functional categories for within-method Q99 off-diagonal genes and proteins")
```
